downside.definition: The negative or bad part of something.
downside.example: The downside of judgement is that it stops us from growing.

judgement.definition: An opinion formed about whether something or someone is good or bad.
judgement.example: Judgement of yourself can be harmful.

bugbear.definition: Something that annoys or bothers you a lot.
bugbear.example: Good parent/bad parent labels are my bugbear.

labels.definition: Words or names used to describe or categorize someone.
labels.example: Labels like 'good parent' or 'bad parent' are not helpful.

extremes.definition: The furthest or most intense points; the very ends of a range.
extremes.example: These labels are about extremes, not reality.

perfectly.definition: In a way that has no mistakes or faults.
perfectly.example: It is impossible to be perfectly attuned all the time.

attuned.definition: Closely aware of and responsive to someone's feelings or needs.
attuned.example: No one can be perfectly attuned to their child.

intentions.definition: Plans or purposes; what you mean to do.
intentions.example: Even good intentions can have bad results.

consequences.definition: The results or effects of an action.
consequences.example: Some good intentions can have harmful consequences.

labelled.definition: Given a name or description, often in a way that is unfair or limiting.
labelled.example: Nobody wants to be labelled a bad parent.

humiliation.definition: A feeling of being deeply embarrassed or ashamed.
humiliation.example: To avoid the humiliation of the bad role, we get defensive.

defensive.definition: Quick to protect yourself from criticism or blame.
defensive.example: We get defensive about our parenting mistakes.

misattuning.definition: Failing to understand or respond correctly to someone's emotional needs.
misattuning.example: We don't examine ways we are misattuning to our children.

neglecting.definition: Failing to care for or give enough attention to something or someone.
neglecting.example: We may be neglecting their emotional needs.

identity.definition: Who you are; the qualities that make you a particular person.
identity.example: She clung to the identity of being a good mother.

parental.example: Parental fear of facing mistakes is common.

pretending.definition: Acting as if something is true when it is not.
pretending.example: Pretending your child's feelings don't matter causes harm.

shaming.definition: Making someone feel shame or deep embarrassment.
shaming.example: It feels too shaming to admit our faults.

faults.definition: Mistakes or weaknesses in a person.
faults.example: We cannot improve if we can't admit our faults.

attributes.definition: Qualities or characteristics used to describe someone.
attributes.example: Let's drop good and bad as attributes for parents.

wholly.example: No one is wholly saint or sinner.

saint.definition: A very good and holy person.
saint.example: No parent is wholly a saint.

sinner.definition: A person who does wrong things.
sinner.example: No parent is wholly a sinner either.

grumpy.example: A grumpy but honest parent may be better than a fake one.

frustrated.definition: Feeling annoyed because you cannot do what you want.
frustrated.example: A frustrated parent hiding behind sweetness is worse.

resentful.definition: Feeling bitter and angry about something you think is unfair.
resentful.example: A resentful parent hiding behind a sweet facade is harmful.

facade.definition: A false front that hides what is really behind it.
facade.example: She hid her frustration behind a facade of sweetness.

syrupy.definition: Excessively sweet or sentimental in a way that is not real.
syrupy.example: Her syrupy sweetness was not genuine.

restriction.definition: A limit or rule that controls what you can do.
restriction.example: It is hard to thrive with the restriction of a label.

thrive.definition: To grow strong and healthy; to do very well.
thrive.example: Children need freedom from labels to thrive.

clumsy.definition: Moving in an awkward way; tending to drop things or fall.
clumsy.example: Don't label a child as 'the clumsy one'.

noisy.definition: Making a lot of loud sound.
noisy.example: Calling a child 'the noisy one' is a limiting label.

describe.definition: To tell what something is like using words.
describe.example: Describe what you see rather than judging.

appreciate.definition: To recognize the value or good quality of something.
appreciate.example: Say what you appreciate about their effort.

concentrating.definition: Giving all your attention to one thing.
concentrating.example: I liked how hard you were concentrating.

sums.definition: Simple math problems, like addition or subtraction.
sums.example: She was concentrating hard on her sums.

impressed.definition: Feeling admiration because of how good something is.
impressed.example: I am impressed by how much thought you put into this.

drawing.definition: A picture made with a pen or pencil.
drawing.example: She put a lot of thought into her drawing.

praise.definition: Words that say someone did a good job.
praise.example: Praise effort rather than giving a general judgment.

effort.definition: The physical or mental energy you use to do something.
effort.example: Praise the child's effort, not just the result.

encouraging.definition: Giving support, hope, or confidence to someone.
encouraging.example: Describing what you see is more encouraging than just saying 'great job'.

specific.definition: Clearly defined; about one particular thing.

criticism.definition: Saying what is wrong with something or someone.
criticism.example: Specific praise is more useful than criticism.

untidy.example: The page of writing was nearly an untidy mess.

neatly.definition: In a clean, tidy, and organized way.
neatly.example: I like how neatly you've written that letter.

formed.definition: Shaped or made in a particular way.
formed.example: The letter P was perfectly formed.

hopefully.definition: Used to express a wish that something will happen.
hopefully.example: Hopefully, next time you'll notice another letter too.

suspending.example: Try suspending judgement rather than deciding right away.

conclusions.definition: Final decisions or opinions reached after thinking about something.
conclusions.example: Hold your conclusions lightly rather than rigidly.

lightly.definition: Without too much force or seriousness; gently.

rigidly.definition: In a stiff way that does not allow change.

harm.definition: Damage or hurt caused to someone or something.
harm.example: This approach will do less harm to everyone.

matchless.definition: So good that nothing else can compare; unique.
matchless.example: Every child is a matchless mix of genes and environment.

genes.definition: The basic units passed from parents to children that determine physical and other qualities.
genes.example: Children are shaped by their genes and environment.

environment.definition: The conditions and people around you that affect how you grow.
environment.example: You are a major part of your child's environment.

responsibility.definition: The duty to take care of something or someone; being accountable.
responsibility.example: Take responsibility for how you react to your children.

overlooked.example: These important aspects of parenting are often overlooked.

examining.definition: Looking at something carefully to understand it.
examining.example: It is easier to examine children's behavior than our own.

personality.definition: The qualities and traits that make a person who they are.
personality.example: Your reactions shape your child's personality.

traits.definition: Qualities or characteristics of a person's character.
traits.example: Personality traits are shaped by environment.

character.definition: The combination of qualities that makes someone who they are.
character.example: A child's character is shaped by their experiences.

witness.example: Children are shaped by what they witness at home.

convinced.definition: Made someone feel certain that something is true.
convinced.example: I hope I have convinced you to examine your reactions.

judgemental.definition: Too quick to form negative opinions about others.
judgemental.example: Try to be less judgemental about yourself.

observe.definition: To watch carefully and pay attention to what is happening.
observe.example: Instead of judging, observe what you do right.

banning.example: I'm not totally banning the words 'good' or 'bad'.

yoga.definition: A practice of physical exercises and breathing that improves body and mind.
yoga.example: I've improved at yoga since last week.

baking.definition: Cooking food in an oven, especially bread or cakes.
baking.example: Concentrating on my baking is paying off.

paying off.example: All your hard work is paying off.

improved.example: I have improved at yoga since last week.

behaviours.definition: Ways of acting or conducting yourself.
behaviours.example: We focus on children's behaviours rather than our own.

shapes.definition: Influences and forms something over time.
shapes.example: How we respond to children shapes who they become.

respond.definition: To react or reply to something.
respond.example: How we respond to children matters.

affect.definition: To have an influence on; to change or impact.
affect.example: How your children affect you is worth examining.

rupture.definition: A break or tear in a relationship caused by hurt.
rupture.example: Mistakes matter less when you repair the rupture.

repair.definition: To fix something that was broken or damaged.
repair.example: Changing your behavior can repair the rupture.
